skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: A nationwide survey of residents in all 50 states asked whether they exercise daily. The percentages of residents who exercise daily for 7 of the states are 18.10%, 19.30%, 20.50%, 21.40%, 22.50%, 23.30%, 24.20%. The median percent for all 50 states is 22.10%. What is the difference between the median percent for these 7 states and the median percent for all 50 states?
rationale: For the 7 listed values in ascending order, the median is the 4th value: 21.40%. The given all-states median is 22.10%. The positive difference between these medians is 22.10 − 21.40 = 0.70%.
